description_long: This is a large, ornate metal kettle resembling a traditional Russian samovar, with a rounded, bulbous body and a slightly tapered top section. The surface is metallic with a worn, aged patina, giving it a vintage appearance. Two side-mounted, arched handles with dark grips extend from the upper body, while a short, angled spout protrudes from one side near the base. The lid is circular, slightly domed, and topped with a small, round knob, appearing to be removable. The kettle stands on a sturdy, four-legged footed base, which elevates it above the ground. There are no visible decorative patterns, but the surface texture and patina add visual interest.
description: A large, vintage metal kettle with a rounded body, arched side handles, a short spout, and a domed lid with a knob, standing on a four-legged footed base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both arched side handles and the rounded body, with the domed lid and footed base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the short, angled spout and a rectangular hatch or door on the body, with one handle visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing both handles and the rounded body, but the spout is not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the spout and rectangular hatch visible, showing the other side handle.
